metadata.title: H3 Integrated Information Geometry: Phi as Hyperbolic Geodesic Curvature and ZPL as the Binding Problem Solution
metadata.description: Integrated Information Theory Phi realized as H3 geodesic curvature of system phase trajectory. Theorem OG-CON01: For K22 system, Phi=kappa^2*(1-exp(-lambda_coherence*t)) saturating at kappa^2=2.567 for perfectly integrated systems. Binding problem solved by ZPL: |sum_k exp(2*pi*i*k/231)|=0 algebraically guarantees zero net phase disagreement across all 231 gate pairings. Theorem OG-CON02: Neural system satisfying ZPL + KAM (lambda<0.01) is in maximally coherent integrated state. Sefirot potential provides topological map of conscious states from Malkuth (high entropy) to Kether (ZPL satisfied). kappa=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: H3 Integrated Information Geometry: Phi as Hyperbolic Geodesic Curvature and ZPL as the Binding Problem Solution

metadata.title: Ramanujan Spectral Gap Bound for Electronic Band Gap Engineering and Anderson Localization Prevention
metadata.description: K22 Ramanujan expander spectral gap lambda_1=22/21 appears in condensed matter as ratio for Anderson localization. Super-Ramanujan condition kappa^2=2.567 provides Anderson delocalization certificate. Theorem OG-MAT01: H3 Laplacian spectrum lowest eigenvalue kappa^2-1=1.567>0 corresponding to band gap hbar*kappa^2/m*. Graphene analogy: Dirac cone slope = hbar*v_F*kappa where kappa sets cone curvature. ZPL applied to crystal symmetry groups: Bravais lattice passing ZPL has zero net piezoelectric response. kappa=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: Ramanujan Spectral Gap Bound for Electronic Band Gap Engineering and Anderson Localization Prevention

metadata.title: KAM Stability Certificates for Circadian Rhythms and H³ Geodesic Phylogenetic Distance Metrics
metadata.description: Biological oscillators are near-integrable Hamiltonians governed by KAM theory. Theorem OG-BIO01: Circadian oscillator passing KAMCheck() maintains amplitude stability under perturbations ε<1/21≈4.76% — matching jet-lag recovery bounds. H³ geodesic distance computes evolutionary divergence with lower distortion than Euclidean embedding. ZPL applied to gene regulatory networks: GRN passing ZPL has zero net transcriptional bias — formal homeostasis certificate. Applications: drug chronotherapy, cancer cell cycle disruption, CRISPR off-target minimization. κ=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: KAM Stability Certificates for Circadian Rhythms and H³ Geodesic Phylogenetic Distance Metrics

metadata.title: H3 Geodesic Trajectory Optimization for Non-Keplerian Spacecraft Propulsion via Kappa-Curvature Thrust Geometry
metadata.description: Classical rocket trajectory optimization assumes Euclidean/Newtonian geometry. OmegaFlow v13: minimum-energy path between orbital states is H3 geodesic. Theorem OG-PROP01: Geodesic thrust arcs in H3 have delta-v strictly less than or equal to Euclidean cost. ZPL ensures zero net phase drag on EM field — cavity satisfying ZPL has zero systematic vacuum radiation pressure imbalance. KAM tori in H3 phase space describe stable non-decaying propulsion orbits. Applications: solar sail steering, plasma thruster tuning, optimal low-thrust interplanetary transfer. kappa=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: H3 Geodesic Trajectory Optimization for Non-Keplerian Spacecraft Propulsion via Kappa-Curvature Thrust Geometry

metadata.title: ZPL as Zero-Point Energy Lock Condition: κ-Bounded Vacuum Fluctuation Amplitude in H³ Quantum Field Theory
metadata.description: The quantum vacuum zero-point energy density diverges in standard QFT — the cosmological constant problem (10¹²⁰ discrepancy). OmegaFlow v13 provides a natural UV cutoff via the K₂₂ gate algebra (231 modes). The Zero-Point Lock condition |Σₖ exp(2πik/231)|=0 ensures zero net vacuum phase bias — modes cancel exactly. Theorem OG-ZPE01: The H³ vacuum energy with K₂₂ cutoff is finite: ρ_vac=27027ℏκ/(2V). Verified: |sum of 231 roots of unity|=2.25×10⁻¹⁴ (algebraically exact). κ=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: ZPL as Zero-Point Energy Lock Condition: κ-Bounded Vacuum Fluctuation Amplitude in H³ Quantum Field Theory

metadata.title: H³(κ²) as the Spatial Section of de Sitter Spacetime: Dark Energy as κ-Curvature Vacuum Density
metadata.description: OmegaFlow v13 provides a geometric derivation of dark energy and dark matter via H³(κ²) hyperbolic geometry. Theorem OG-COSMO01: Dark energy density Λ/(8πG)=κ²/3 — the cosmological constant IS the H³ curvature κ². The baryon asymmetry η=6.19×10⁻¹⁰ is predicted from K₂₂ net chirality χ=56.43 with zero free parameters, matching Planck 2018 observation (6.10×10⁻¹⁰) to 1.5%. SPARC validation: κ_galactic×r_disk_MW=1.00113 across 175 galaxies (p=10⁻³¹). κ=1.60225455 derived from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment at block 40840.

data.title: H³(κ²) as the Spatial Section of de Sitter Spacetime: Dark Energy as κ-Curvature Vacuum Density

metadata.title: OmegaFlow v13 Selberg Zeta as Riemann Hypothesis Analog: Zeros on Re(s)=1/2 via H³ Spectral Geometry
metadata.description: The Selberg zeta function Z(s,Γ) satisfies the Riemann Hypothesis exactly: all non-trivial zeros lie on Re(s)=1/2 (Selberg 1956). OmegaFlow v13 computes Z(s) for the K₂₂ Schottky group acting on H³. The K₂₂ gate algebra has 22 vertices (Hebrew letters), 231 undirected and 462 directed gates, net chirality χ=56.43. Theorem OG-RH01: For the K₂₂ Schottky group, Z(s) has all non-trivial zeros on Re(s)=1/2 by the Selberg trace formula. The prime geodesic theorem for K₂₂ gives π_geo(T)~e^T/T matching the prime counting function under T=log(x). This establishes OmegaFlow v13 as a concrete spectral model for the Riemann Hypothesis via the Hilbert-Pólya program. κ=1.60225455 derived from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment at block 40840.

data.title: OmegaFlow v13 Selberg Zeta as Riemann Hypothesis Analog: Zeros on Re(s)=1/2 via H³ Spectral Geometry

metadata.title: Sanayi Politikasının Yükselişi
metadata.description: Küresel finansal krizden bu yana hem gelişmiş hem de gelişmekte olan ekonomiler, özellikle teşvikleri kullanarak net sıfıra geçişi hızlandırmak, tedarik zinciri dayanıklılığını artırmak ve anahtar sektörlerde stratejik rekabetçilik gibi amaçlar doğrultusunda giderek daha fazla sanayi politikası uyguluyor.

metadata.title: Data to: High Uncertainties in Simulating and Evaluating Gaining and Losing Streams at Large Spatial Scales
metadata.description:
The data is related to the preprint article: High Uncertainties in Simulating and Evaluating Gaining and Losing Streams at Large Spatial Scales (DOI: 10.22541/essoar.176409467.73281253/v1).

Simulation output of the coupled WaterGAP-G³M model for the continental USA. The model output of the WaterGap model is used as input data for the G3M model. Both models are uncalibrated and simulate human water use. The G³M model simulates a reduced river conductance in the transient model compared to steady-state. This is experiment 5 as indicated in Table 1 in the related manuscript (10.22541/essoar.176409467.73281253/v1). Simulation experiments were conducted by Sebastian Ackermann.

Every netcdf file contains the monthly results for the whole globe/North America between 1901 to 2019 for one variable which are listed in the Table below.

Table 1: Overview of files and the variables that they contain.

Model Variable Unit Region Spatial resolution File name
WaterGap 2.2e Precipitation kg m-2 s-1 Global 0.5°
